title: Intel and AMD Sign Long-Term Server CPU Deals with Chinese Clients as Prices Surge
summary: Intel and AMD are signing long-term server CPU procurement agreements with Chinese data center clients, locking in supply for one to two years amid price surges of over 40% since early 2025. This indicates that AI demand is tightening server CPU supply, significantly raising costs for Chinese cloud and internet companies scaling AI operations. The agreements typically lock in volume but not price, and some clients are negotiating two-year terms. Monthly CPU price increases in China have exceeded 10% recently.
context: AI accelerators, such as GPUs and NPUs, are specialized hardware for AI workloads, but general-purpose server CPUs remain essential for data center infrastructure. The AI boom has increased demand for both, causing CPU supply constraints as foundries prioritize higher-margin accelerator chips. Chinese firms, heavily investing in AI, are particularly affected by these supply tightness and price hikes.
